lede: A look at September 10 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Giovanni Gronchi and Jérémy Toulalan, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Operation Barras
events.desc: Operation Barras successfully frees six British soldiers held captive for over two weeks and contributes to the end of the Sierra Leone Civil War.

events.title: 1961 Italian Grand Prix
events.desc: In the Italian Grand Prix, a crash causes the death of German Formula One driver Wolfgang von Trips and 15 spectators who are hit by his Ferrari, the deadliest accident in F1 history.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The submarine HMS Oxley is mistakenly sunk by the submarine HMS Triton near Norway and becomes the Royal Navy's first loss of a submarine in the war.

events.title: Nyon Conference
events.desc: Nine nations attend the Nyon Conference to address international piracy in the Mediterranean Sea.

events.title: George Mary Searle
events.desc: George Mary Searle discovers the asteroid 55 Pandora.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: Nathan Hale volunteers to spy for the Continental Army.

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: Johann Sebastian Bach leads the first performance of Jesu, der du meine Seele, BWV 78, a chorale cantata based on a passion hymn by Johann Rist.

events.title: Nagasaki
events.desc: Fifty-five Christians are executed in Nagasaki during the Great Genna Martyrdom.

events.title: 1509 Constantinople earthquake
events.desc: An earthquake known as "The Lesser Judgment Day" hits Constantinople.

births.name: Giovanni Gronchi
births.line: Italian soldier and politician, 3rd President of the Italian Republic

births.name: Charlie Waters
births.line: American football player, coach, and radio host

deaths.text: Emil Julius Gumbel, German mathematician and statistician
Wolfgang von Trips, German race car driver
Letitia Christian Tyler, American wife of John Tyler, 11th First Lady of the United States
Émilie du Châtelet, French mathematician and physicist
Louis I of Hungary, King of Hungary and Croatia from 1342 to 1382
Qin Shi Huang, first emperor of China (born 259 BC)

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Thomas Tsugi, Charles Spinola, and Martyrs of Nagasaki
Christian feast day: Edmund James Peck (Anglican Church of Canada)
